questions.text: When you buy something — whether it's a new gadget, clothing, or something for your space — how much does sustainability factor into your decision? Are there specific brands you trust, types of products you avoid, or certifications you look for? Or is it more of a 'if it works and feels right, I'll get it' approach?

When it comes to weather and climate — you mentioned warm, walkable cities — but do you have strong preferences around humidity, rainy seasons, or altitude? Like, does living somewhere humid bother you, do you avoid places with long rainy winters, or do you not really notice?

When you think about your personal growth or philosophy — like how you make sense of life, purpose, or big questions — do you lean on a specific framework (Stoicism, Buddhism, something else), is it more of a mix-and-match personal belief system, or do you not really think about it in those terms?

When special occasions come up — your birthday, holidays, celebrations with friends or Remi's gotchas — how do you like to mark them? Do you have specific traditions, prefer low-key vs. big gatherings, or are you the type to make a big deal out of milestones?

When it comes to planning and organizing your personal life — aside from work tasks in Linear — do you use any specific tools or methods (bullet journals, Notion, apps, whiteboards), or is it mostly in your head or in notes? How do you keep track of non-work goals, reminders, or life admin?
